key_features: Comprehensive CRUD operations with tenant isolation via organizationId
Paginated query support with customizable sorting and filtering
Order number generation with sequential padding and date-based prefixing
Item management with create, update, delete operations
Status transition support for order lifecycle
Type-safe interfaces for all data models
Default pagination limits (20 per page, max 100)
Soft delete support via notDeleted filter
Status workflow management with state machine logic
Order lifecycle operations: create, confirm, prepare, deliver, complete, cancel
Price calculations with subtotal, tax, discount handling

patterns_discovered.name: Repository Pattern with Tenant Isolation
patterns_discovered.description: All database operations require organizationId parameter, enforcing multi-tenant data segregation at query level
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents cross-tenant data leakage, ensures data consistency

patterns_discovered.name: Status Workflow Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Order lifecycle managed through defined status transitions (PENDING → CONFIRMED → PREPARED → DELIVERED → COMPLETED/CANCELLED)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear state machine prevents invalid status transitions

patterns_discovered.name: Pagination with Bounds Checking
patterns_discovered.description: Pagination parameters (page, perPage) validated and normalized with min/max limits
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents performance issues from unreasonable pagination requests

patterns_discovered.name: Type-Safe Data Transfer Objects
patterns_discovered.description: Separate interfaces for create (CreateOrderData), update (UpdateOrderData), and read operations
patterns_discovered.benefit: Compile-time type safety, clear API contracts

patterns_discovered.name: Parallel Query Execution
patterns_discovered.description: Count and data queries executed in parallel using Promise.all

patterns_discovered.name: Sequential Order Numbering
patterns_discovered.description: Order numbers generated with pattern: PREFIX-DATEPREFIX-SEQUENCE (e.g., ORD-20260201-0001)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Human-readable, sortable, unique per organization per day

patterns_discovered.name: Selective Relation Loading
patterns_discovered.description: findByIdWithRelations allows specifying which relations to include (items, table, customer, feedback)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents N+1 queries and over-fetching of data

patterns_discovered.name: Soft Delete Support
patterns_discovered.description: notDeleted filter applied to repository queries
patterns_discovered.benefit: Maintains referential integrity while preserving historical data

gotchas_discovered.description: getNextOrderNumber extracts sequence from order number split by '-', assumes exactly 3 parts minimum
gotchas_discovered.risk: If order number format changes or corrupted number exists, parsing may fail or return NaN
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add validation for parts.length and isNaN checks before incrementing sequence

gotchas_discovered.description: findAll accepts dateFrom and dateTo as parameters but doesn't validate date format or relative ordering
gotchas_discovered.risk: Invalid date strings or dateTo < dateFrom could produce unexpected results
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add date validation and throw appropriate error for invalid ranges

gotchas_discovered.description: Invalid pagination values are silently corrected without notifying client
gotchas_discovered.risk: Client may assume requested page/perPage but received different values without notification
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider returning actual pagination values in response metadata (appears to be done)

gotchas_discovered.issue: Order Number Uniqueness Not Enforced at Repository Level
gotchas_discovered.description: orderNumber uniqueness is organizational scope but no unique constraint mentioned
gotchas_discovered.risk: Concurrent order creation could generate duplicate order numbers
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure database unique constraint on (organizationId, orderNumber) tuple

gotchas_discovered.issue: Status Transition Validation Missing at Repository Level
gotchas_discovered.description: Repository accepts any status in UpdateOrderData without validating transitions
gotchas_discovered.risk: Service must enforce all business rules; repository allows invalid transitions if called directly
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider adding validation in repository or ensure service layer is the only entry point

gotchas_discovered.description: totalAmount stored and retrieved as numeric type, potential rounding issues
gotchas_discovered.risk: JavaScript Number type has precision limits for currency; should use Decimal for financial calculations
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify Prisma schema uses Decimal type and ensure all calculations use Decimal arithmetic

approach_outcome.description: Successfully implemented Order repository and service with complete status workflow
key_achievements: Comprehensive repository layer with 900+ lines covering all CRUD operations
Full order lifecycle state machine (PENDING → CONFIRMED → PREPARED → DELIVERED → COMPLETED/CANCELLED)
Flexible pagination and filtering with bounds protection
Sequential order number generation with date-based organization
Item-level status management alongside order status
Event notification system for status changes
approach_outcome.implementation_quality: High - Well-structured with clear separation of concerns, comprehensive documentation, consistent patterns
approach_outcome.completeness: Subtask fully implemented with both repository and service layers

recommendations.suggestion: Verify Prisma schema has unique constraint on (organizationId, orderNumber) to prevent duplicate order numbers in concurrent scenarios
Audit all numeric type declarations for currency fields; use Prisma Decimal type instead of regular number for financial calculations to prevent precision loss
Add defensive validation in getNextOrderNumber to handle malformed order numbers gracefully with fallback logic
Add validation in findAll method for dateFrom/dateTo parameters to ensure valid dates and proper ordering (dateFrom ≤ dateTo)
Consider indexing on (organizationId, status), (organizationId, createdAt), and (organizationId, orderNumber) for frequently-filtered queries
Create branded types for OrderId, OrderNumber to prevent accidental misuse at compile time
Consider adding explicit response codes or error details to repository methods to improve error handling in service layer
Implement comprehensive test coverage for status transitions, edge cases in order number generation, and concurrent operation handling
